paper.title: Variable hybridization outcomes in trout are predicted by historical fish stocking and environmental context

analysis_design.primary_P2: Trout: 58 fish from the sampled site nearest Buffalo Bill Reservoir
guardrails: Candidate C is an ecological introduction-history prior, not pedigree or exclusive-edge truth.
Both trout taxa were stocked and long-term hybridization/backcrossing makes temporal direction non-unique.
The main P1, P2, and P3 cohorts use completely disjoint sequencing-library sets.
Published site ancestry summaries reuse the same SNPs and cannot be independent labels.
The released SNPs were globally filtered at at least 60% data and MAF above 5% using all fish, including scored cohorts; later locus filters also use panel labels.
Seven representation/filter/reference/target rows are correlated sensitivities, not trials.

release_discrepancies: Dryad version 2 lists every one of nine paths twice with identical size and MD5; importers must deduplicate by path and digest.
Per-individual Entropy q/Q values and ancestry classes are absent; only site-level response summaries are archived.
The released vcf2mpgl.pl replaces parsed PL values with 0 0 0 whenever GT is ./., discarding informative masked likelihoods.
Corrigendum DOI 10.1111/mec.15381 is confirmed, but its publisher-hosted correction content was unavailable and remains substantively unaudited.
